transcript:
Right now you're watching a wireframe torus tumble slowly, with a few yellow dots crawling along its grid lines. Watch the four-second mark, when it turns edge-on — the donut hole vanishes and the whole thing flattens into a hollow tube. That's the piece's argument happening in real time.

Those yellow dots aren't decoration — in the code they're called probes, and a comment says they're "particles that attempt to penetrate, but cannot." Watch the offset variable: when a probe pushes deep enough, the code doesn't stop it — it flips the angle called v by adding PI and sends it back out the other side. The probe never finds an interior, it just re-emerges as surface. And the projection is orthographic on purpose — the comment notes "no perspective, no depth hierarchy," so nothing ever reads as behind anything else.

So the shape isn't hiding a hole and revealing it through rotation — the hole and the solid were always the same flat fact, just rotated into a different reading.
